title: 1981-04-16_Pat_83.txt
key_claims: Rudolf Otto's comparison of Shankara and Meister Eckhart validates Dick's experience. Eckhart's "fourth level of the soul" (the *scintilla* or divine spark) allows a person to see things abstractly as they pre-exist as forms in God's intellect.
The realization of Atman-Brahman requires a higher rational faculty (intelligence), not just emotion. Order, intelligence, and abstraction are directly proportional.
The cosmic dialectic is a self-winding clock that constantly accrues a negentropic surplus. This takes the form of reticulated, arborizing complexity (Boehme's *sic/non*).
Seeing the world abstractly places one in the "antechamber" of experiencing God, reversing the ontological fall and returning multiplicity to unity.
recurring_concepts: **Pretextual Cause**: The observation that in the divine schema, the effect is greater than the cause, indicating ex nihilo newness being fed into the system (Christ as organizing principle).
**Four Axes of Unity**: Dick realizes his vision achieved unity on four fronts simultaneously: self, spatiotemporality, objects, and inner-outer reality.
reading_excerpt: Rudolf Otto's comparison of Shankara and Meister Eckhart validates Dick's experience. Eckhart's "fourth level of the soul" (the *scintilla* or divine spark) allows a person to see things abstractly as they pre-exist as forms in God's intellect.
